behavior_summary: Implements a guardsman NPC's perception, area-trigger, scheduler, cleanup, and interaction behaviors.
GUARD2::look (perception/heartbeat) announces the guard with a short bark; it chooses a "dead guardsman" line when the NPC is flagged dead and a normal "guardsman" line otherwise.

GUARD2::enterFastArea runs when an actor enters the guard's fast trigger: if the guard is dead it clears dead/combat state and teleports the guard into position (revive+reposition); otherwise it frees the temporary info created for the trigger and spawns a short on-enter handler (GUARD2::0C02).
GUARD2::leaveFastArea frees the temporary info created for the fast-area trigger.
GUARD2::schedule is a periodic scheduler that spawns movement/action child methods (METHOD::143A and optionally METHOD::133F) and waits on their results.

GUARD2::use is a multi-branch dialog interaction: it presents an ask-list, varies replies with randomness and global flags, sets per-guard state flags (guard2Met, guard2GaveDirections, guard2Pissed), can spawn pointing/movement methods to show directions, and performs temporary UI/timed spawns/cleanup.
notable_control_flow: GUARD2::look: set per-call info/exclude -> Npc::isDead() test -> Item::bark("dead guardsman" | "guardsman") -> imply/suspend on bark result -> return.
U8P_GUARD2::enterFastArea: set info/exclude -> Npc::isDead() ? (clrDead, clrInCombat, teleport to fixed coords) : (call FREE::2A56 to free info and spawn U8P_GUARD2::0C02).

U8P_GUARD2::use: initial bark and small spawned helper(s) -> build slist and Item::ask -> switch on returned string -> update global flags (guard2Met, guard2GaveDirections, guard2Pissed) and possibly spawn pointing/movement methods -> free slist and return.
U8P_GUARD2::schedule: spawn METHOD::143A and suspend until completion; depending on returned value spawn METHOD::133F.
open_questions: Exact text/content for many dialog lines is represented by inferred literals (e.g., "dead guardsman", "guardsman"); the authoritative strings are not present here.

Precise behavior and parameters of spawned helper routines (GUARD2::0C02, METHOD::143A, METHOD::133F, METHOD::1090, FREE::1C19) are inferred by call site patterns but their implementations are not in this file.
Meaning/semantics of some engine utility calls (FREE::1C19, FREE::2A56) are inferred as cleanup/free functions but could include additional side effects.
Exact coordinates/direction/floor used in Npc::teleport in enterFastArea are known to exist but their in-world significance (patrol start, spawn anchor) is inferred.

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD2::look
main_function.purpose: Perception/heartbeat handler that responds to a U8P_GUARD2::look/perceive event by checking whether the guard is dead and barking an appropriate short identification line; it sets temporary info/exclusion for the caller.
entry_conditions: Event 0 'U8P_GUARD2::look' (triggered when an actor/player examines or the NPC's perception tick occurs).
Called with pid of the actor/process that triggered the U8P_GUARD2::look and the guard's process pointer.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD2::look
function_map.role: Perception/heartbeat handler that barks an identification line and branches on Npc::isDead().

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD2::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area entry trigger: revive+teleport dead guard or free trigger info and spawn an on-enter handler when guard is alive.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD2::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit trigger: cleanup/free temporary info created for the fast-area interaction.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD2::schedule
function_map.role: Periodic scheduler that spawns movement/action methods and conditionally spawns follow-up methods based on child results.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD2::use
function_map.role: Player 'use' (talk/interact) handler implementing a dialog tree, state flags updates, random reply variation, and direction-pointing behavior via spawns.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD2::0C02
function_map.role: Short on-enter handler spawned from enterFastArea when guard is alive; exact actions inferred (likely short greeting or short animation).

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn short on-enter handler when a non-dead actor enters fast area.
Spawn a child method to perform periodic movement/action; schedule waits on its result.
Optionally spawn a follow-up method depending on the result returned by METHOD::143A.
Spawn movement/pointing to show directions to the player (called from dialog branches).
Spawn a small timed/utility cleanup action during interaction (exact effect unclear).

external_calls.purpose: Test whether this NPC is flagged as dead to choose the bark line.
Make the NPC speak a short identification line to the triggering process/player.
Clear the NPC's dead flag during the revive path when a player enters the fast area.
Clear in-combat state as part of reviving/resetting the guard.
Teleport the revived guard to fixed coordinates and facing/destination (reposition after revival).
Free/cleanup the temporary info/list created when setting up the fast-area trigger (used in non-revive branch).
Present the player with a short list of dialog choices and receive the selected string to branch conversation.
Choose between multiple dialog replies randomly to add variation to responses.
Emit dialog lines to the interacting player for many branches in the interaction tree.
Determine direction to a target item/location so the guard can point or spawn movement toward it (used in direction-giving branches).

reads.name: hydrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Influences dialog branching in use() by incrementing a local counter used to select a catastrophic/Titans branch.

reads.name: pyrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Influences dialog branching in use() by incrementing the same local counter as hydrosFree.

reads.name: hasHeart
reads.likely_effect: Read in use() to help decide between Titan-related dialog vs dead/zombie branch.

reads.name: guard2Met
reads.likely_effect: Read to select first-time introductory dialog vs repeated dialog in use().

reads.name: guard2Pissed
reads.likely_effect: Read to pick annoyed vs neutral responses; some branches clear or set it to alter future tone.

reads.name: guard2GaveDirections
reads.likely_effect: Read to determine whether to re-give directions to the player; prevents repeats if already given.

reads.name: benticMet
reads.likely_effect: Used to conditionally add a warning/wary dialog line in the interaction tree.

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Selects which name (Devon vs alternate) the guard cites when asked about duty.

writes.name: guard2Met
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the player first asks 'Who are you?', marking that the guard has met the player.

writes.name: guard2Pissed
writes.likely_effect: Cleared/set in dialog branches to change the guard's annoyance state for subsequent interactions.

writes.name: guard2GaveDirections
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the guard gives directions so future queries skip re-giving them.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements a guardsman NPC's perception, area-trigger, scheduler, cleanup, and interaction behaviors.
